sourcePosture: Official company investor relations materials reviewed where available; portfolio weights come from data/latest-portfolio.json, the app's freshest reference portfolio snapshot.
limitations: Consensus estimate timestamps were not loaded, so this product focuses on reported results, company guidance, and PM judgment rather than beat-versus-consensus math.
Full transcript/Q&A mapping is only loaded where directly reviewed; other holdings rely on earnings releases and official result pages.
Portfolio prices, position sizes, and P/L use data/latest-portfolio.json, which the app refreshes from the freshest available portfolio payload.

portfolioView.verdict: Recent post-print evidence still favors your AI infrastructure cluster, but the real portfolio risk is concentration and clean-EPS quality in the cloud platform names rather than an obvious thesis break.
portfolioView.action: Hold the core AI infrastructure winners, treat MU on 2026-06-24 as the next hard catalyst, and re-underwrite GOOGL and AMZN on operating income rather than headline EPS.

NVDA.verdict: Cleanest large-weight print in the book. The quarter reinforced the AI infrastructure leadership thesis and did not rely on an obvious below-the-line EPS distortion.

epsQuality.note: GAAP and non-GAAP EPS diverge, but the release also showed strong revenue, gross margin, and data center growth, so the headline result looks directionally supported by operating performance.

highlights: Revenue was $81.6 billion, up 85% year over year.
Data Center revenue was $75.2 billion, up 92% year over year.
GAAP diluted EPS was $2.39 and non-GAAP diluted EPS was $1.87.

managementEvidence.summary: Management framed the quarter around accelerating AI factory buildouts and agentic AI demand rather than a one-off product cycle.

nextCatalyst.label: Next earnings date
nextCatalyst.note: The official investor relations event pages reviewed on 2026-06-14 did not yet show the next earnings date.

GOOGL.verdict: Operating momentum was strong, especially in Search and Cloud, but headline EPS was flattered by a large other-income gain, so this print should be judged on revenue, margin, and cloud backlog rather than net income alone.

epsQuality.note: Other income included a $37.7 billion net gain, primarily unrealized gains on non-marketable equity securities, which materially inflates net income and EPS.

highlights: Revenue was $109.9 billion, up 22% year over year, or 19% in constant currency.
Google Cloud revenue was $20.0 billion, up 63% year over year.
Operating margin expanded to 36.1%.

managementEvidence.summary: On the earnings call, management said AI is driving Search usage, Cloud backlog nearly doubled quarter over quarter to more than $460 billion, and Gemini Enterprise paid monthly active users grew 40% quarter over quarter.

nextCatalyst.label: Next earnings date
nextCatalyst.note: No later earnings date was visible on the official pages reviewed on 2026-06-14.

MU.verdict: This remains one of the best operating-leverage names in the portfolio, but it is also the next real catalyst, so the current setup is less about re-rating past results and more about whether June 24 confirms another step-up.

epsQuality.note: The available official release materials point to operating and cash-flow strength rather than a below-the-line driven EPS beat.

highlights: Fiscal Q2 2026 revenue was $23.86 billion versus $13.64 billion in the prior quarter and $8.05 billion in the year-ago period.
Adjusted free cash flow was $6.9 billion.
Management said it expected exceptional records across revenue, gross margin, EPS, and free cash flow in fiscal Q3.

managementEvidence.summary: Management framed memory as a strategic AI bottleneck and guided to stronger fiscal Q3 performance.

nextCatalyst.label: Fiscal Q3 2026 results
nextCatalyst.note: Official IR notice set the fiscal third-quarter earnings call for June 24, 2026.

AVGO.verdict: Broadcom is giving you one of the clearest source-backed AI follow-through signals in the portfolio, with accelerating AI semiconductor revenue and explicit forward commentary into Q3.

epsQuality.note: The release emphasis was revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and AI semiconductor revenue growth rather than a one-off below-the-line EPS driver.

highlights: Q2 FY2026 revenue was $22.187 billion, up 48% year over year.
Q2 AI semiconductor revenue was $10.8 billion, up 143% year over year.
Adjusted EBITDA was 69% of revenue.

managementEvidence.summary: Management said the momentum continues and expects AI semiconductor revenue to reach $16.0 billion in Q3, up more than 200% year over year.

nextCatalyst.label: Next earnings date
nextCatalyst.note: The official Broadcom pages reviewed on 2026-06-14 did not yet list the next earnings date after the June 3, 2026 call.

AMZN.verdict: AWS and operating income were strong, but the headline net income number materially benefited from Anthropic-related gains, so this is another position where the operating read matters more than reported EPS.

epsQuality.note: First-quarter 2026 net income included $16.8 billion of pre-tax gains in non-operating income from Amazon's investments in Anthropic.

highlights: Q1 2026 net sales were $181.5 billion, up 17% year over year.
AWS sales were $37.6 billion, up 28% year over year.
Operating income was $23.9 billion versus $18.4 billion in Q1 2025.

managementEvidence.summary: Management highlighted AWS as the fastest growth in 15 quarters and said the custom chips business topped a $20 billion annual revenue run rate.

nextCatalyst.label: Q2 2026 company guide
nextCatalyst.note: Amazon guided Q2 2026 net sales to $194.0 billion to $199.0 billion, or 16% to 19% growth year over year.

TSM.verdict: TSMC remains the cleaner foundry confirmation of continued AI demand, with strong first-quarter margins and a higher second-quarter revenue guide.

epsQuality.note: The official quarterly result page emphasized revenue and margin guidance rather than unusual below-the-line items.

highlights: 1Q26 net revenue was $35.90 billion.
1Q26 gross margin was 66.2% and operating margin was 58.1%.
2Q26 revenue guidance was $39.0 billion to $40.2 billion.

managementEvidence.summary: The official quarterly results page showed a clean step-up in Q2 guidance for revenue and margins.

nextCatalyst.label: Next earnings date
nextCatalyst.note: The source pack used here included the 1Q26 results page and financial calendar references, but no explicit next earnings date was captured.

AMD.verdict: The position is small, but the print was directionally supportive: Data Center is now the main growth engine and Q2 guidance still points higher.

epsQuality.note: The release aligned revenue, operating income, and EPS improvement without an obvious below-the-line distortion.

highlights: Q1 2026 revenue was $10.3 billion, up 38% year over year.
Operating income was $1.5 billion and GAAP diluted EPS was $0.84.
Q2 2026 revenue guidance was approximately $11.2 billion, plus or minus $300 million.

managementEvidence.summary: Management said AI infrastructure demand accelerated and that server growth should accelerate meaningfully as supply scales.

nextCatalyst.label: Q2 2026 company guide
nextCatalyst.note: Management guided Q2 2026 revenue to about $11.2 billion, plus or minus $300 million, with non-GAAP gross margin around 56%.

SMCI.verdict: The latest quarter repaired margin and year-over-year revenue growth, but the name still carries execution and working-capital risk that is less attractive than your higher-quality AI infrastructure positions.

epsQuality.note: The result page showed gross-margin recovery and guided revenue, but also showed cash flow used in operations, which matters more than headline EPS here.

highlights: Q3 FY2026 net sales were $10.2 billion versus $12.7 billion in Q2 FY2026 and $4.6 billion in Q3 FY2025.
Gross margin improved to 9.9% from 6.3% in Q2 FY2026.
Q4 FY2026 revenue guidance was $11.0 billion to $12.5 billion.

managementEvidence.summary: Management said the transformation into a total datacenter infrastructure provider is accelerating and linked the margin recovery to DCBBS growth.

nextCatalyst.label: Q4 FY2026 guide
nextCatalyst.note: The company guided Q4 FY2026 GAAP diluted EPS to $0.53 to $0.67 and non-GAAP diluted EPS to $0.65 to $0.79.
